prompt: A finished 16:9 editorial still, retro 16-bit pixel-art (SNES/Genesis era) cut-and-assembled like a paper collage; flat aged-yellow paper field (#C9A876) with subtle uncoated paper fiber and faint CRT scanlines; chunky pixels with ordered dithering, thin warm-cream keylines around each cut pixel-sprite group, soft low-opacity drop shadows; horizontal 16:9 locked frame, subject in middle 70 percent, generous flat color-field negative space, 3 to 6 large separable pixel groups for assemble-from-empty. Visual proposition: a pixel paper-craft map close-up: the south-eastern corner of the South American continent occupies the left side, a tiny cluster of small islands (an archipelago of two main islands) sits in the pixel sea to its right; one VERY SHORT solid warm-cream measuring arc connects the mainland coast to the small islands; a second EXTREMELY LONG warm-cream DASHED arc starts at the same small islands and stretches far toward the upper-right corner, running off the edge of the frame; both arcs share identical line thickness so the short-versus-long contrast is stark. Constraint: one short arc and one very long arc leaving the frame, same stroke width, dramatic length contrast, ABSOLUTELY NO labels, no letters, no numbers on the map. Avoid: no typography, no readable letters, no numerals, no logos, no team crests, no jersey names or numbers, no real recognizable faces, no photoreal, no glossy 3D, no camera UI, no watermark, no clutter.
